title: entitlement-consumed event — v1 wire contract
description: FROZEN wire shape for the `entitlements::EntitlementConsumed` event. Emitted afterCommit on the EntitlementUsage row for every successful consume(). High-volume in production — subscribers should be careful about downstream write amplification. Every consumer reads this shape unchanged; breaking changes require a v2 per the versioning policy in `foundation/readme.md` §12.

x-versioning.policy: Same policy as tenant-provisioned.v1 — additive changes stay in v1; breaking changes create v2 with parallel dispatch. Because this event is high-volume, breaking changes should be avoided; prefer new sibling events (e.g. `entitlement-approached-cap`) over v2 bumps.

x-volume: high (~10-100 events / tenant / hour on active tenants)

entitlement_id.description: The resolved Entitlement row for this (tenant, key) tuple.

usage_id.description: The EntitlementUsage audit row primary key.

kind.description: Kind of the entitlement being consumed.

quantity.description: How many units this consume() call spent. Almost always 1; higher for bulk operations.

period.description: Reset window for `pool` kind. Null for `slot` / `boolean` / `unlimited`.

used_total.description: Total used INCLUDING this consume() call, within the current period.

value_cap.description: Cap for the current period. Null when kind=`unlimited` or `boolean`.

remaining.description: Cap minus used_total. Null for unlimited. Zero means the caller just spent the last unit — subsequent consume() will raise EntitlementExceeded.

period_started_at.description: When the current period began (for pool kind).

period_resets_at.description: When used_total resets to 0 (for pool kind).

consumed_at.description: When the EntitlementUsage row was written.

correlation_id.description: Optional caller-supplied token linking multiple consume() calls for a single logical operation (e.g. a bulk send batch).

actor.description: Who caused the consume. Optional — background jobs may omit.
